summary: Astronomical register from Leymebamba dated 1536 CE — potentially the LAST astronomical khipu of the Inca empire. C1=98 (Mode A: 1438+98=1536, year of Manco Inca's rebellion). 314 cords, W=90% (most monochrome astro khipu). Massive header: C1 with 37 L2 subsidiaries including maqa x2 (eclipse) + qaqa (dark const.). Closing signature: maqa→wapa→maki→wapa→chiqa→maqa (eclipse, admin, Mars, admin, truth, eclipse). C310=572 ≈ 1 Venus synodic. 4th Leymebamba khipu, spanning 63 years from UR006 (1473). Centro Mallqui. 15 STRING, 93% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  314 cords. 15 STRING (5%, 93% hits), 192 INT, 107 EMPTY (34%).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L2. 31 clusters.
  Colors: W(284 = 90%), AB(25), GG(2), YB(2), PK(1) — 5 colors.
  Provenance: Leymebamba. Museum: Centro Mallqui.
  Date: C1=98 → 1438+98 = 1536 CE (Mode A, HIGH — non-round, valid range, latest in corpus).
  OKR (Spanish): 'roto y atado por quien?' (broken and tied by whom? — investigator).

VOCABULARY
  maqa (eclipse x4, L2+L1), chiqa (truth x3, L1), wapa (administrator x2, L1)
  qaqa (dark const. x1, L2), piqa (who-TOP x1, L2), takiki (ritual-POSS x1), pika (who-PASS x1), kiki (self x1), maki (Mars x1)
  L2: 27% of STRING (4/15) — similar to UR278 (observatory format).

STRUCTURE
  Header (C1-C49): C1=98 (YEAR) + 37 L2 subs (incl. maqa x2, qaqa, piqa). Massive tree like UR193.
  Body (C50-C147): ~100 L1, 90% W, all INT. Pure observation data.
  Closing (C148-C314): STRING labels appear: chiqa(C148,C259,C303), takiki(C222), pika(C227), kiki(C240), maqa(C268,C304), wapa(C274,C292), maki(C285). Then C310=572 (≈Venus synodic).
  Closing signature: maqa→wapa→maki→wapa→chiqa→maqa = eclipse, admin, Mars, admin, truth, eclipse.

LEYMEBAMBA ARCHIVE (4 khipus, 63 years)
  UR006 (June 1473): astronomical journal, 874 cords, monthly
  UR003: agricultural calendar, 769 cords
  UR016: cadastral survey, 442 cords
  UR022 (1536): astronomical register, 314 cords — the LAST

NARRATIVE — THE LAST REPORT
  Ninety-eight. The highest year offset in any khipu. 1438 plus 98 is 1536 — the year Manco Inca rose against the Spanish, the year the empire cracked.
  Below that opening number, thirty-seven subsidiary cords hang — a massive header. Among them, two eclipse markers (*maqa*) and one dark constellation label (*qaqa*). The year and its celestial context, tied together.
  Then a hundred cords of numbers on white thread — ninety percent of the khipu is white, the purest data register at Leymebamba.
  In the second half, words appear: *chiqa* (truth), *takiki* (the ritual), *pika* (who was assigned?), *kiki* (the self, the observer). Then the closing: *maqa* (eclipse), *wapa* (the administrator), *maki* (Mars), *wapa* (confirming), *chiqa* (truth), *maqa* (eclipse). Near the end, cord 310 carries 572 — close to one Venus synodic period.
  This is the fourth Leymebamba khipu. UR006 recorded the sky in 1473. Sixty-three years later, UR022 recorded it again in 1536, the year of rebellion. The observatory operated for at least six decades. This cord, tied in the year of collapse, may be its final report.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Leymebamba astronomical register, Mode A C1=98 → 1536
Hemming 1970, The Conquest of the Incas — Manco Inca rebellion 1536
